title: Auxiliary stop-horizon target distilled back into next-lap AUC model
summary: A recent pit-stop decision paper reports gains from coupling next-lap classification with a stop-horizon regression head.
rationale: A recent pit-stop decision paper reports gains from coupling next-lap classification with a stop-horizon regression head. For this task, predicting whether the next stop is 1, 2, 3, 4-6, or 7+ laps away can force smoother sequential structure than raw binary labels alone.
implementation_hint: Create a training-only target for laps until next PitStop event per Race-Year-Driver, bucket it into ordinal classes, train an auxiliary OOF model, and feed only its OOF class probabilities or expected horizon into the final binary model. Validate by race-year groups so the auxiliary target never leaks across folds.
expected_effect: Adds a softer notion of pit-window proximity, reducing brittle dependence on hand-tuned tire-life thresholds.
risk: If the auxiliary model is weak, it can just recycle existing features and add noise. Must avoid using future-derived features directly at inference.
